question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile whose items all carry consensusTimestamps outside the DateRangeFilter window so that dateRangeFilter.filter(recordItem.getConsensusTimestamp()) returns false for every item, causing recordItemListener.onItem() to never be called and all transactions in the file to be silently suppressed from the mirror node database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile containing a single RecordItem with a consensusTimestamp equal to exactly DateRangeFilter.start - 1 nanosecond, exploiting the inclusive boundary check (timestamp >= start && timestamp <= end) to cause that transaction to be excluded from recordItemListener.onItem() while still being accepted by aggregator.accept(), leading to a discrepancy where the transaction affects block-level aggregates but is never persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile with a consensusEnd value far in the future so that the RecordFileParsedEvent is published with an inflated consensusEnd before any items are processed, causing downstream event listeners to believe the node is ahead of its actual state and thereby suppressing re-gossip of transactions that have not yet been ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated @Retryable retries by causing recordItemListener.onItem() to throw a transient exception, and because parserContext.clear() is called in the finally block on every attempt, cause the parserContext state to be wiped before the @Transactional commit, resulting in transactions being permanently lost from the database even after all retry attempts succeed at the listener level?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile that causes the @Transactional timeout (configured via recordParserProperties.getTransactionTimeout()) to be exceeded during doParse(), resulting in a transaction rollback that silently drops all RecordItems in that file without any retry mechanism catching the timeout exception, preventing those transactions from ever being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a List<RecordFile> where the second file has a mismatched previousHash, causing a HashMismatchException to be thrown after the first file's items have been passed to recordItemListener.onItem() but before doFlush() is called, so that the first file's transactions are processed in-memory but never committed to the database because the @Transactional rolls back on the exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldParse() (AbstractStreamFileParser)] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile whose consensusStart is less than or equal to the previous file's consensusEnd, triggering the 'Skipping existing stream file' warn path and causing shouldParse() to return false, thereby silently dropping all transactions in that file without any error being raised or retry attempted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldParse() (AbstractStreamFileParser)] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the consensusEnd of a previously stored RecordFile in the streamFileRepository so that getLast() returns a file with an artificially high consensusEnd, causing all subsequently submitted RecordFiles to be skipped via the 'previous.getConsensusEnd() >= current.getConsensusStart()' check, permanently preventing gossip of all future transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a v6 RecordFile with a manipulated index value such that the computed offset (recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1) is a large negative number, causing repository.updateIndex(offset) to corrupt all existing block indices in the database, making previously gossiped transactions unreachable by block number and effectively suppressing their visibility?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with version >= VERSION (6) immediately after a v5 file where the index difference is zero, causing the offset to be zero and updateIndex() to skip the migration, leaving all v5 block numbers incorrect and making those transactions inaccessible via block-number-based queries, effectively preventing their gossip to consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile with an empty items list (getItems() returns an empty iterable), causing count to remain 0, recordFile.setCount(0) to be set, and aggregator.update() to produce zero gas/bloom, so that the file is committed as an empty block that silently swallows the expected transactions without any error or retry?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where aggregator.accept(recordItem) is called for all items but dateRangeFilter.filter() excludes all of them, so that aggregator.update(recordFile) still sets gasUsed and logsBloom on the RecordFile from the excluded items, creating a state inconsistency where the block appears to have contract activity but no transactions are persisted via recordItemListener.onItem()?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on parse(RecordFile) by submitting an extremely large RecordFile that takes a very long time to process, holding the monitor lock and blocking all other parse() calls, effectively creating a denial-of-service that prevents any other transactions from being gossiped for the duration of the lock hold?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a List<RecordFile> where only the last file passes shouldParse(), causing doFlush() to be called only on the last file while all intermediate files' transactions are processed via doParse() but never committed because the @Transactional rolls back if the last file fails, dropping all intermediate transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordItem with a consensusTimestamp of Long.MIN_VALUE or Long.MAX_VALUE, causing dateRangeFilter.filter() to produce an unexpected boolean result due to long overflow in the comparison (timestamp >= start && timestamp <= end), and thereby either force-include or force-exclude that transaction from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: recordMetrics()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordItem with a consensusTimestamp so far in the past that Duration.between(consensusTimestamp, Instant.now()) overflows or produces a negative duration, causing the latency metric recording to throw an exception that propagates out of recordMetrics() and aborts processing of all subsequent items in the forEach loop, preventing those transactions from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a RecordFile whose getItems() returns a stream that throws a RuntimeException mid-iteration (e.g., from a lazy-loaded collection), causing the forEach loop to abort after some items have been passed to recordItemListener.onItem() but before others, and because the @Transactional will roll back on the exception, all partially processed transactions are lost?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the @Retryable mechanism with maxAttempts exhausted by repeatedly causing a retryable exception, and because each retry calls parserContext.clear() in the finally block, ensure that after all retries are exhausted the parserContext is empty and the @Transactional rolls back, permanently preventing the transactions in that RecordFile from being gossiped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile with a very large number of RecordItems (e.g., millions), causing the AtomicLong count and AtomicInteger logIndex to be incremented so many times that the JVM heap is exhausted, triggering an OutOfMemoryError which is excluded from @Retryable retries (excludes = OutOfMemoryError.class), causing the entire file's transactions to be permanently dropped?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordFile where parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars()) is called with a null or malformed sidecar list, causing a NullPointerException that aborts the doParse() method after all items have been processed via recordItemListener.onItem() but before the @Transactional commits, rolling back all transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: shouldParse() (AbstractStreamFileParser)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the hash chain verification bypass when previous.getType().isChained() returns false, submitting a RecordFile with a completely wrong previousHash that passes shouldParse() unchecked, allowing a forged file to be parsed and its transactions to replace or suppress legitimate gossiped transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where recordFile.getConsensusEnd() returns a value that does not correspond to any item's consensusTimestamp, causing the RecordFileParsedEvent to be published with an incorrect consensusEnd that misleads downstream consumers into thinking transactions up to that timestamp have been gossiped when they have not?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: parse(List<RecordFile>)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a List<RecordFile> where all files are skipped by shouldParse() (because initial == previous after the loop), causing the early return at 'if (initial == previous) { return; }' to be hit, so that no doFlush() is called and no RecordFileParsedEvent is published, silently suppressing all transactions in the batch?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a RecordItem whose getTransactionType() returns a value not present in the sizeMetrics or latencyMetrics maps, causing getOrDefault() to fall back to unknownSizeMetric/unknownLatencyMetric, and if those fallback metrics are null (due to a construction race), throw a NullPointerException inside recordMetrics() that aborts processing of all subsequent items in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a RecordFile where the logIndex AtomicInteger is shared across all items via setLogIndex(logIndex), and by injecting a RecordItem that causes logIndex to overflow Integer.MAX_VALUE, corrupt the log indices of all subsequent transactions in the file, making them unqueryable and effectively preventing their gossip to consumers?
